title: UDLM DCM Function Capability Matrix + Role Assignment (ADR-RBAC-001)
description: Two record shapes for DCM RBAC, enforced by the ONE Governance Matrix (no new engine). `role_capability` binds an access-role to the DCM functions it may perform (default-deny: only listed functions permitted). `role_assignment` binds an actor or access-group to a role (optionally tenant-scoped, time-bounded). Roles are access-role TaxonomyTerms; functions are dcm-function TaxonomyTerms. Role-assignment CHANGES are audited as immutable forward events (CAPABILITY_ADMIT-style; SPEC-DESIGN §18a-e) — this record is the current binding. Authored as data so it PROJECTS onto Kessel/SpiceDB (roles->relations, functions->computed permissions) without depending on it.

permitted_functions.description: dcm-function TaxonomyTerm handles this role may perform (e.g. provider.capability.admit). DEFAULT-DENY: a function not listed (nor inherited via a parent function-domain term) is denied. A domain term (e.g. `provider`) grants all its leaves.

subject_type.description: actor = an Identity.Person/ServiceAccount; access_group = a DCMGroup with group_class access_grouping (holds actors — the keystone delta).

subject_ref.description: actor_uuid or DCMGroup uuid (authoritative — never name alone).

role.description: access-role TaxonomyTerm handle granted to the subject.

tenant_scope.description: OPTIONAL — scopes the assignment to one tenant (e.g. tenant_admin over tenant X). Absent = platform-wide.

granted_by.description: The platform_admin actor who made the assignment. REQUIRED — assignment is an audited, admin-gated function (access.role.assign); every grant/revoke is an immutable forward audit event.

expires_at.description: OPTIONAL — time-bounded assignment (reuses DCMGroup membership time-bounds).
